label: Calf strain without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an uncomplicated calf strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a calf strain. We did not find a fracture, tendon rupture, circulation problem, nerve problem, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest the injured area as instructed.
Use ice, compression, elevation, gentle motion, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new swelling, numbness, weakness, color change, or trouble using the limb.
A pop with loss of strength, new deformity, or concern for tendon rupture.
Fever, redness spreading over the area, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated calf strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, severe pain, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, inability to use the limb, or high-risk trauma context.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain home-care framing and warning signs. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed calf strain without fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated calf strain
no fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, or specialist-directed plan documented
